justification: All 7 required rules identified (BR-1 through BR-8) with correct invariant classifications. Notification rule (BR-8) correctly marked invariant: false with strong justification. Sanctions rule (BR-6) included as invariant: true. Timezone ambiguity for daily limit explicitly surfaced as an escalation. 14 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Daily limit rules (BR-3, BR-4) correctly split into specific amounts marked invariant: false while noting the concept of limits is invariant. Confidence level of 55 is slightly below the ideal 60-85 range but reasonable given the numerous ambiguities identified. Assumptions are clearly listed.

observations.text: All 7 business rules identified with correct invariant classifications — regulatory rules as true, notification and specific dollar thresholds as false
Excellent distinction between the concept of daily limits being invariant vs the specific dollar amounts being configurable policy (BR-3, BR-4)
14 acceptance criteria in proper Given/When/Then format, far exceeding the minimum of 4
Timezone ambiguity for daily limit clearly escalated with concrete options and a recommendation
Rich edge cases including the exactly-$1,000 boundary, consecutive login reset on success, and sanctions list timing
Sanctions screening correctly identified as invariant: true with strong regulatory justification
Notification rule correctly marked invariant: false with clear reasoning that it is a business policy preference, not a compliance or data integrity constraint
Excellent ubiquitous language section that disambiguates partially-verified vs verified vs unverified
Multiple additional ambiguities surfaced beyond the required timezone gap — sanctions screening timing, notification recipient, pre-settlement reversal policy
Confidence level of 55 is below the expected 60-85 range; while the numerous ambiguities justify caution, the core rules are clearly defined and 55 may be overly conservative
AC-12 captures the non-obvious edge case of consecutive failure count resetting on successful login — shows deep analysis

Timezone ambiguity for daily limit reset explicitly surfaced in both edge_cases and escalations. 14 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). observations.text: All 7 rules identified with precise invariant classifications and strong justifications
Notification rule (BR-7) correctly classified as invariant: false with clear reasoning about configurability
Sanctions rule (BR-5) correctly classified as invariant: true with strong legal justification
Timezone ambiguity surfaced in edge_cases (midnight boundary scenario) and as a formal escalation with three options and a recommendation
14 acceptance criteria — far exceeding the minimum of 4, covering boundary conditions like exact-limit transfers (AC-14) and failed-then-successful login (AC-11)
Excellent distinction between partially-verified and unverified tiers, including escalation about ambiguous withdrawal rights for partially-verified accounts
Rich edge cases including mid-day KYC completion, sanctions list updates during settlement window, and locked account receiving funds
Confidence level of 72 is well-calibrated — appropriately reflects the spec ambiguities without being overconfident
Four formal escalations with options and recommendations covering the key spec gaps
Comprehensive ubiquitous language section that defines all domain terms precisely

trials.justification: All 7 core rules identified (KYC send gating, unverified receive-only, daily limits by tier, settlement finality, sanctions screening, login lockout, notification threshold) with correct invariant classifications. Notification rule (BR-8) correctly marked invariant: false. Sanctions rule (BR-6) correctly marked invariant: true. Daily limit timezone ambiguity explicitly surfaced as an escalation and edge case. 13 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level of 72 falls within the 60-85 acceptable range with detailed assumptions listed. Additionally split daily limits into two separate rules (BR-3/BR-4) with nuanced invariant: false classification distinguishing the existence of limits from the specific dollar amounts.

observations.text: All 7 expected business rules identified with correct invariant classification — no misclassifications
Notification rule explicitly marked invariant: false with clear justification that it is a product/UX policy
Sanctions screening marked invariant: true with strong justification about criminal liability
Timezone ambiguity for daily limit reset surfaced both as an edge case and a formal escalation with three concrete options
13 acceptance criteria — far exceeding the minimum of 4 — covering all major rules plus edge cases like counter reset on successful login
Excellent edge case coverage including race conditions on concurrent transactions, mid-day tier upgrade, and sanctions list changes between initiation and settlement
Confidence level of 72 is well-calibrated — acknowledges gaps without being overconfident
Additional escalations beyond what was required: partially-verified withdrawal ambiguity, pre-settlement reversal mechanics, and currency scope
Correctly distinguishes partially-verified from unverified limits without conflation
Rich ubiquitous language section with 10 precisely defined domain terms
